original_hypothesis.title: Admin misconfiguration of configuredTokens.decimals > 18 can revert getLockedWeightedValue(), disrupting reward accounting that depends on it
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: getLockedWeightedValue computes `10 ** (18 - decimals)`. If any configured token has decimals > 18, the subtraction underflows and reverts, potentially breaking downstream reward distribution or other protocol operations that require this call.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While primarily configuration-driven, it can become a production outage if rewards contracts rely on getLockedWeightedValue and there is no fallback. This is especially relevant given docs expect support for very high decimals.
deployment_assumptions: Admin configures at least one token with decimals > 18
A critical state-changing path in another contract calls getLockedWeightedValue (or uses it as a prerequisite) such that the revert blocks operations
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Test script configures decimals=18; no evidence for production configs or any downstream caller dependencies.

proof_of_signal.pattern: deltaDecimal = 10 ** (18 - configuredTokens[token].decimals); underflow if decimals>18 (Solidity 0.8.x reverts)

preconditions: At least one configured token has decimals > 18
Some operational workflow depends on getLockedWeightedValue not reverting

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: A single bad token config can break weighted-value computations for all users (loop reverts).

operational_questions: Is getLockedWeightedValue used by any state-changing reward distribution function elsewhere?
Is there a validation step on configureToken to bound decimals to <=18?
validation_requirements: Static scan: find all call sites to getLockedWeightedValue in the system
Config test: configure decimals=19 and confirm revert; assess impact on dependent operations

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Any dependent workflow calling getLockedWeightedValue begins reverting.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Potential protocol-wide outage if weighted values are required for rewards/eligibility.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low to medium—can be fixed by re-configuring decimals, but may require governance process.
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Reward computations and any component relying on weighted locked value

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: TokenConfigured events setting decimals>18; spikes in reverts from getLockedWeightedValue
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Single config change correlated with outage

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
